original_abstract: Learning probabilistic models that produce well-calibrated predictions is essential for effective decision-making in artificial intelligence. In this paper, we introduce Bayesian Binning into Quantiles (BBQ), a novel non-parametric calibration method designed to overcome the primary limitations of existing techniques. BBQ operates as a post-processing step for binary classification outputs, allowing it to be easily integrated with a wide range of established learning algorithms. Through an extensive series of experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets, we demonstrate that our method is computationally efficient and provides superior empirical accuracy compared to traditional calibration approaches.
humanized_abstract: Uncertainty estimation for well-calibrated predictive models is a crucial aspect in most applications of artificial intelligence. In this paper, we introduce Bayesian Binning into Quantiles (BBQ) – a non-parametric post-processing method to calibrate probabilistic models. BBQ overcomes many of the existing challenges associated with other calibration methods, and as a post-processing method for the output of a base binary classifier, it can be seamlessly applied to almost all machine learning models. We present extensive experimental comparisons with other calibration methods on simulated and several benchmark real-world datasets. Our results show that the BBQ model is computationally efficient, and often provides the most accurate calibration performance.
